sections.label: THINKING
sections.content:
The game says "game over" but the level still shows N blocks. It seems like I clicked too many N blocks at once or the game has a limit on actions per turn. Let me check the current state and see what happened. The image still shows the same level with N blocks intact, so maybe the clicks didn't register properly or there's a different mechanic.
Wait - looking at the image, the N blocks are still there. The game says "game over" which might mean I failed the level. Let me check the result and see if I need to restart or if there's a different approach.
Actually, looking more carefully at the image, the N blocks are still visible. Maybe the clicks didn't work because the game has a different mechanic - perhaps I need to click them one at a time, or navigate the player to them first.
Let me check the last action result to understand what happened.

sections.label: TOOL CALL: python
sections.content:
<tool_call>
<function=python>
<parameter=code>
# Check what happened
print(f"Last action result: {last_action_result}")

# Check current state
seg = current_frame.segmentation
n_blocks = [n for n in seg['nodes'] if n['color'] == 'N']
print(f"N blocks remaining: {len(n_blocks)}")

# Check if game is actually over or just the level
print(f"Level: {current_frame.level}, Step: {current_frame.step}")

# Maybe I need to click N blocks one at a time
# Let me try clicking just one
if n_blocks:
    n = n_blocks[0]
    center_r = sum(p[0] for p in n['boundary']) // len(n['boundary'])
    center_c = sum(p[1] for p in n['boundary']) // len(n['boundary'])
    print(f"Clicking one N at ({center_r}, {center_c})")
    action([{'action': 'MOUSE', 'row': center_r, 'col': center_c}])
</parameter>
</function>
</tool_call>
